title: Adelphydraena orchymonti Perkins 1989
author: Perkins, Philip D.
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-zero
description: Adelphydraena orchymonti  Perkins   Figs 1, 3, 13 (habitus), 10 (aedeagus), 14 (map), 17–18 (habitat)   Adelphydraena orchymonti  Perkins 1989: 450   Differential diagnosis.  The smallest member of the genus (ca. 1.24 mm), differentiated from other  Adelphydraena  by the combination of small size, the non-chordate pronotum, the very small metaventral plaques (Fig. 13), and the aedeagus (Fig. 10).   New collection records:   Venezuela:  T. F. Amazonas, Puerto Ayacucho (40km S), El Tobogan, Canocoromoto, 26Jan1989, stream edge at upper shelter, collected by P. J. Spangler, R. A. Faitout, & C. B. Barr (1 NMNH). Amazonas prov., 100m, Tobogan de la Selva, S Puerto Ayacucho, 24- 25.1.2004, M. Balke (4 specimens, NMW, IBE); one female used for DNA extraction and sequencing (specimen voucher MNCN-AI356). Amazonas prov., Cerro Cuau env., 19.11. 2012, 923 m a.s.l., 05°05’39.1’’ N, 67°27’41.4’’ W, F. Čiampor, Loc 30 (1 female, IBE). Used for DNA extraction and sequencing (specimen voucher IBE-AN711).   Remarks.  The habitus images (Figs. 3, 13) and the aedeagus drawing (Fig. 10) are of the specimen collected at El Tobogan, Canocoromoto.
